channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nairobi nutritionist quits stable job after seminar, starts successful clinic in Mlolongo with one employee after five months
item.description: Keruchi Derrick, a young nutritionist based in Nairobi, made a bold decision to quit his well-paying job five months ago. Despite earning a decent salary, he felt drained by long working hours, tight ...

item.title: Canadian consumer confidence falls to 11-month low as Iran war lifts energy prices
item.description: Canadian consumer confidence slipped to its lowest level in nearly a year as the war in Iran continued into its sixth week, raising energy prices and increasing concerns about persistent inflation. Th...

item.title: Kanye West to Perform at Wireless Festival Despite Past Controversial Comments
item.description: Wireless Festival organisers have confirmed that Kanye West will headline the upcoming event in London, despite widespread criticism of his previous antisemitic remarks. Melvin Benn, managing director...

item.title: ACT’s illegal tobacco market booming amid weak enforcement
item.description: Across the ACT, a sophisticated black market for illegal cigarettes is flourishing, largely unnoticed by authorities. While legitimate packs cost up to $62, illegal versions are being sold for as litt...

item.title: Seven English stroke centres still do not provide 24/7 thrombectomy services
item.description: Despite repeated government assurances, not all stroke patients in England have access to mechanical thrombectomy 24/7. This minimally invasive procedure can remove clots in severe strokes, potentiall...

item.title: Trump reveals details of dramatic rescue mission for downed US airman in Iran
item.description: Donald Trump has shared extraordinary details about a high-risk rescue mission to extract a US airman who had been stranded in southern Iran for nearly 48 hours. The rescue operation involved 155 airc...

item.title: Family Calls for Return of Eddie Carvery’s Trailer at Africville Site
item.description: The trailer of Eddie Carvery, the late Africville activist, was removed from the historic Africville site in Halifax over Easter weekend, prompting outrage from his family. Carvery, who passed away in...

item.title: Two people killed in Siaya after protests over alleged forced evictions by gold mining company
item.description: On Monday in Gem–Ramula, Siaya County, two people were shot dead during protests against Shanta Gold Kenya Limited, which villagers claim is trying to evict them from ancestral land. Police confirmed ...

item.title: Families express concern over Girlguiding’s new policy on transgender members
item.description: From September, Girlguiding will require all transgender girls and young trans women volunteers to relinquish their memberships, following a supreme court ruling interpreting 'sex' in the Equality Act...

item.title: Diesel Prices to Surge Again on Tuesday
item.description: According to reports, diesel prices in the Philippines are expected to increase significantly again this coming Tuesday. This marks another round of price hikes for fuel, which has been impacting cons...

item.title: President Ruto’s visit to GCA Headquarters underscores Kenya’s commitment to climate action
item.description: President William Ruto paid a visit to the Global Center on Adaptation (GCA) headquarters in Rotterdam, Netherlands, ahead of the Africa Climate Summit scheduled to take place in Nairobi. His tour was...

item.title: Mitchell Kemboi: Close Friend Mourns and Shares Heartfelt Messages After Tragic Accident
item.description: Mitchell Kemboi, the daughter of Kenya Ports Authority boss Captain William Ruto, tragically lost her life in a road accident in Taita Taveta after their car collided with a stationary trailer. The in...

item.title: Assam CM's Allegations Against Congress and Other Key Political Developments
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has alleged that a Pakistani social media group supplied the documents which Congress used to accuse his wife of holding multiple foreign passports. He also cl...

item.title: Trump Proposes $1.5 Trillion Defense Budget with Cuts to Non-Defense Spending
item.description: US President Donald Trump has proposed a major increase in the country's defense budget for the 2027 fiscal year, suggesting a rise of $500 billion, bringing total defense spending to approximately $1...

item.title: Inside LG’s Rollable Prototype: Why Rollable Smartphones Never Reached the Market
item.description: A recent teardown of LG’s never-released Rollable smartphone reveals why rollable phones never became a mainstream reality. According to Ars Technica, the concept, though visually stunning and innovat...

item.title: How European institutions still influence the restitution of Africa’s cultural heritage
item.description: Theophilus Khumomotse’s opinion piece explores how Europe’s private institutions continue to exercise control over Africa’s cultural and human heritage long after colonial rule formally ended. He argu...

item.title: Family Claims Delhi Assembly Intruder Sarabjit Singh Is Mentally Unstable
item.description: The reported incident involves Sarabjit Singh, a man who allegedly breached the security of the Delhi Assembly. According to the details referenced in the unavailable NDTV article, Singh's unexpected ...

item.title: Three concertgoers hurt after safety fence collapses during Super Junior show in Seoul
item.description: During Super Junior’s concert in Seoul, South Korea, three fans sustained injuries after a safety fence in the audience area collapsed. According to initial reports, the incident happened while the gr...

item.title: Advance voting closes as crucial federal byelections near that could determine Liberal majority
item.description: Advance voting for three federal byelections in Canada ended on Monday, April 6, 2026. The contests are taking place in two Ontario ridings — Scarborough Southwest and University–Rosedale — and one in...

item.title: Commuters' Network gives free meals to help jeepney drivers affected by modernization issues
item.description: A commuters’ advocacy group in the Philippines has started a free meals program to support jeepney drivers who continue to face challenges due to the government’s public utility vehicle (PUV) moderniz...
